task_description: Build the Threat Intelligence Engine for ThreatOne Enterprise Desktop - Phase 6. Create src/threat_intel/ module with IOC Manager, Threat Feed Manager, Feed Parsers, MITRE ATT&CK Integration, CVE Database, Threat Correlation Engine, IOC Matching Engine, Threat Scoring, Intel Report Generator, and Enrichment Service. Integrate with SecurityEngine and add comprehensive unit tests.

verification.summary: Full build succeeds. All 380 tests pass after review fixups. Fixed: recursive shared_lock UB in matchAll, full matcher rebuild in ingestFeed, non-atomic initialized_ flag, STIX rfind quote mis-parse, and per-query domain lowercasing overhead.

issues_fixed: Recursive shared_lock UB in IOCMatcher::matchAll - split into public+private _impl methods
Full matcher rebuild in ingestFeed - replaced with incremental addIOC for new IOCs only
initialized_ not atomic - changed to std::atomic<bool>
STIX rfind quote mis-parse - now finds value quote after '= ' marker
Domain per-query tolower - store pre-lowercased domains at insert time
issues_not_fixed: SecurityEngine unconditionally allocates full threat intel stack (issue 6) - deferred as architectural decision
goto in ThreatCorrelation (issue 7) - low severity style issue, not a bug
OpenIOC XML parsing namespace/CDATA-unaware (issue 8) - acknowledged as intentional given no XML library
